t_en: As you inspect these items again, you realize that they are no longer a scattered bunch of boxes but a great monument. No one can remove a single stone from this great monument, as it is now the foundations of a great ship.

t_ch: 墙角堆着三个快递箱。依照你的良好习惯，你决定看看它们都是来自何处、去向何方的。
t_en: There are three delivery boxes piled up in the corner. As you are a good person, you decide to take a look at where they are from and where they are going.

t_ch: 你凑近一看，才发现这三个破旧的箱子并非快递。箱子上赫然写着「信仰质量理论实验用具」几个大字。
t_en: As you approach, you realize that these old boxes are not actually courier parcels. There is a note on them that reads: "Items for the faith mass theory experiment" in large letters.

t_ch: 你隐约记得「信仰质量理论」这个概念，好像是丹恒给你科普的。简单来说，就是富集了人类信仰的物件，会在人的主观感受上更重。
t_en: You vaguely remember the concept of the "faith mass theory." You believe it was Dan Heng who taught you about it. Put simply, it states that items filled with the reverence and faith of humanity will subconsciously feel heavier.

t_ch: 在很多学者眼中，这不仅仅是心理作用，而是人心影响到物质世界的例证。这项研究是当世显学，相关学者每年都能获得不少研究津贴。
t_en: Many scholars believe that this is not only a psychological effect, but actually evidence that the mind can influence the physical world. This research is currently an extremely popular scientific discipline and scholars in this area receive generous grants year after year.

t_ch: 眼前的三个箱子上分别写着：「铁块（1斤）」、「铁块（1斤）」和「帝弓遗物（1斤）」。
t_en: The three boxes in front of you read: "Iron Block (1 kilo)," "Iron Block (1 kilo)," and "Reignbow's Relic (1 kilo)."

t_ch: 按照「信仰质量理论」，即使三者质量相同，「帝弓遗物」对仙舟民来说也明显更重一些。
t_en: According to the "faith mass theory," even if the three items are of the same mass, Xianzhou citizens would perceive the Reignbow's Relic to be significantly heavier.

t_ch: 真的吗？我试试看。
t_en: Really? Let me try.

t_ch: 你依次拿起三个箱子，逐一比较它们的重量。写着「帝弓遗物」的那个箱子出奇得重，重到远远超出了你理解中「主观认知带来的偏差」。
t_en: You pick up the three boxes in turn, comparing their weight. The box with "Reignbow's Relic" written on it is far heavier than expected. That's a much larger weight difference than you anticipated from a "deviation caused by subjective perception."

t_ch: 如果箱子上的标志没有错，那么这三个东西应该都只有1斤重。也许的确是「信仰」赋予了帝弓遗物更大的质量。
t_en: If the signs on the crates are correct, the three items should all weigh 1 kilo each. Perhaps it really is "faith" that caused the relics to have a heavier weight.

t_ch: 也许标志有误，拆箱看看吧。
t_en: Maybe the sign is wrong. Have a look inside the boxes.

t_ch: 你拆开了两个标记着「铁块（1斤）」的箱子，里面各安静地躺着一个普通的铁块。然后你拆开了标记着「帝弓遗物（1斤）」的箱子。
t_en: You opened the two boxes with "Iron Block (1 kilo)" written on them. Inside each, you find a regular iron block. Then, you open the box marked with "Reignbow's Relic (1 kilo)"...

t_ch: 箱子里面空无一物，只有一张字条——
t_en: You find that the box is completely empty except for a single note...

t_ch: 「我知道会有人格外好奇，拆箱检查。这个箱子中空无一物，但它就是比那几个铁块要重。为什么？」
t_en: "I knew that people will grow curious and open this box to check. This box is completely empty, but is still heavier than the boxes with iron blocks. Why is that?"

t_ch: 「因为人们相信这个箱子中装着『帝弓遗物』，而『信仰』是有质量的。」
t_en: "This is because people believe that this box contains the Reignbow's Relic. Faith has weight."

t_ch: 「我希望这个实验能彻底打消您的疑虑，相信这一理论的真实性——我们的心灵活动，可以广泛地影响到物质世界。」
t_en: "I hope that this experiment serves to dispel any doubt and that you will accept our theory — that the activities of our souls and minds can affect the world around us on a large scale."

t_ch: 世界可真奇妙啊…
t_en: The world is truly miraculous...

t_ch: 尽管刚才所发生的一切都有违常识，但铁一般的事实摆在你眼前，你不得不接受。
t_en: Although what just happened wildly contradicts common sense, you are faced with iron-clad proof and have no choice but to accept it.

t_ch: 你重新封好三个箱子，将它们摆回原处，带着对科学的敬畏转身离去了。
t_en: You seal up the three boxes and return them to their original place, and turn to leave with a new respect for science.

t_ch: 这箱子里肯定还有东西…
t_en: There must be something else in this box...

t_ch: 你无论如何都无法相信眼前的现象，索性把心一横，用球棒对着这个空箱子狠狠砸了下去。
t_en: You just cannot bring yourself to accept the phenomenon in front of you. You throw caution to the wind and whack the empty box hard with your bat.

t_ch: 「呛啷」一声，球棒打击到了一个硬物，将你的虎口震得生疼。你捡起箱子，撕开打击位置上覆盖着的纸壳……
t_en: With a great "twang," your bat strikes a hard object and rattles your hand painfully. You pick up the box and tear back the paper covering the area you struck...

t_ch: …纸壳下面是一块又薄又重的金属板。
t_en: ...You find a thin, heavy metal plate underneath the paper.

t_ch: 看起来「信仰质量理论」的确是当世显学，用它赚来的津贴一定不少。
t_en: It seems that this "faith mass theory" is indeed a popular scientific discipline. People must have used it to earn many a large grant...

t_ch: 无聊，都是些迷信。
t_en: Boring. This is all superstition.

t_ch: 如果一个现象只能在人的主观体验中发生，那不就是心理作用吗？你才不信这些「学者」的胡说八道，他们无非是为了骗些研究经费罢了。
t_en: If a phenomenon only works under the influence of the human subconscious, doesn't that make it no more than a psychological placebo effect? You refuse to believe these so-called scholars. They're just trying to scam grant agencies for money.

t_ch: 于是你留下那三个箱子，转身离去了。
t_en: So, you leave the three boxes behind, turn around, and walk away.

t_ch: 都是迷信，我没什么兴趣。
t_en: It's all superstition. I'm not interested.

t_ch: 如果一个现象只能在人的主观体验中发生，那不就是心理作用吗？你才不信这些「学者」的胡说八道，他们无非是为了骗些研究经费罢了。
t_en: If a phenomenon only works under the influence of the human subconscious, doesn't that make it no more than a psychological placebo effect? You refuse to believe these so-called scholars. They're just trying to scam grant agencies for money.

t_ch: 于是你留下那三个箱子，转身离去了。
t_en: So, you leave the three boxes behind, turn around, and walk away.

t_ch: 地上摆着一个敞开的快递箱。你打算看看这是谁遗落在这里的，却没在上面找到单据。应该贴着单据的地方贴着一张纸条，上面如是写着——
t_en: An open package lies on the ground before you. You want to see who has left it behind, but there is no receipt on it. You find a note where the receipt should be attached, stating:

t_ch: 「此处乃是金人巷未解之谜：午夜谜箱。」
t_en: "This is the unsolved mystery of Aurum Alley: The Midnight Mystery Boxes."

t_ch: 「这几个看似平平无奇的纸箱比金人巷的历史还要悠久。没人知道谁制造了它们，也没人知道它们从何而来。」
t_en: "These seemingly normal and unassuming cardboard boxes have actually been around for longer than the Aurum Alley itself. No one knows who created them, or where they came from."

t_ch: 「我们只知道，就算它被人盗走或损毁，第二天午夜还是会原封不动地出现在这里。而那些对纸箱下手的人，全都遭到了厄运。」
t_en: "All we know is that, even if they are stolen or destroyed, they will reappear intact here at midnight the next day. What's more, those that moved or damaged the boxes will all met their doom."

t_ch: 「人们惊讶地发现，只要在其中投入金银首饰或其他贵重物品，就能获得祈福，未来的人生一片顺利。」
t_en: "To people's shock, they realized that if they placed gold, silver, jewelry, or other precious items inside the boxes, their future lives will be blessed and full of good luck."

t_ch: 「而那些对箱中财物出手的人，也无一例外遭到了厄运。」
t_en: "Whereas those who attempt to poach the treasures inside the boxes will, without exception, meet their doom."

t_ch: 你又看了看箱内，发现里面已经积累了不少贵重物品。
t_en: You look inside the boxes again and realize that there are a number of precious items already piled inside.

t_ch: 我也祈个福吧…
t_en: I'll ask for good fortune, too...

t_ch: 你投入了一些财物，并虔诚祈愿「午夜谜箱」护佑你未来的开拓之路万事顺遂。
t_en: You place some money inside and make a devout prayer that the Midnight Mystery Boxes would protect you in your future Trailblaze journey.

t_ch: 没人知道「午夜谜箱」是否满意你的供奉，但每天趁着夜色回收箱中财物的那小子应该挺满意的。
t_en: No one knows if the Midnight Mystery Boxes are satisfied with your offerings, but the man that comes to collect all the treasures under the cover of night every day must be very satisfied.

t_ch: 骗人也走点心吧，这什么玩意。
t_en: What a lazy con. This is ridiculous.

t_ch: 你秉承着理性思考的原则，对这个莫名其妙的「午夜谜箱」视若无睹。
t_en: You stick to your principles of rational thinking and ignore these mysterious Midnight Mystery Boxes.

t_ch: 没人知道「午夜谜箱」是否对此感到失望，但每天趁着夜色回收箱中财物的那小子应该挺失望的。
t_en: No one knows if the Midnight Mystery Boxes are disappointed, but the man that comes to collect all the treasures under the cover of night every day must be very disappointed.

t_ch: 哈哈，这些宝贝都归我了！
t_en: Haha, these treasures are all mine!

t_ch: 你狠狠搜刮了一番，将「午夜谜箱」中的贵重物品统统收入了囊中。
t_en: You mercilessly plunder all the valuables from the Midnight Mystery Boxes and stuff them into your bag.

t_ch: 是夜，金人巷里又多了一个伤心的骗子。
t_en: That night, the streets of Aurum Alley saw yet another heartbroken conman.
